topic.title: Freight Unit Building Rules & Freight Unit Types

learningObjectives.title: Understand FUBR
learningObjectives.description: Explain how Freight Unit Building Rules convert ERP logistics documents into transportation demand.

learningObjectives.title: Understand split and consolidation logic
learningObjectives.description: Describe when demand is split for constraints and consolidated for planning efficiency.

learningObjectives.title: Understand planning sequence
learningObjectives.description: Clarify that freight unit building occurs before optimizer-based transportation planning.

sections.content: Freight Unit Building Rules (FUBR) determine how SAP TM converts ERP logistics documents into Freight Units. These rules control whether transportation demand should be split, merged or grouped based on business requirements such as destination, shipping conditions, delivery dates and transportation constraints.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Freight Unit Building Rules define HOW transportation demand is created before transportation planning begins.

SAP TM automatically splits Freight Units whenever transportation constraints cannot be satisfied. Common reasons include vehicle capacity limits, hazardous material restrictions, incompatible products and different delivery dates.
Compatible Freight Units can be consolidated into larger shipments during transportation planning to improve vehicle utilization and reduce transportation costs.

A company creates one outbound delivery containing frozen food and electronic equipment. The Freight Unit Building Rule separates these products into different Freight Units because they require different transportation conditions and vehicle types.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Design Freight Unit Building Rules based on business constraints rather than manually creating Freight Units. Well-designed rules improve optimizer performance and reduce manual intervention.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A pharmaceutical company configured Freight Unit Building Rules to automatically separate temperature-controlled medicines from normal products. This ensured regulatory compliance while improving transportation planning accuracy.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Certification questions often ask: 'What controls the creation of Freight Units?' The correct answer is Freight Unit Building Rules (FUBR), not the optimizer.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Freight Unit Building occurs before transportation planning. The optimizer works on Freight Units after they have already been created.

items: Freight Unit Building Rules create transportation demand.
Rules determine how ERP documents become Freight Units.
Freight Units can be split based on transportation constraints.
Compatible Freight Units are consolidated during planning.
Freight Unit Building is a prerequisite for transportation planning.
